title: Mandating Environmental Monitoring Programs for Salmonella in Food Facilities
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     contamination     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     globally  ,   with     significant     public     health     and     economic     impacts  .   Environmental     monitoring     programs   (  EMPs  ) are   crucial     for     detecting     contamination   in   food     production     areas     before     it     reaches     consumers  .   Mandating     EMPs     for     Salmonella   in   food     facilities     ensures     early     detection  ,   timely     intervention  ,   and     compliance     with     food     safety     regulations  .   Such     programs     can     identify     contamination     hotspots  ,   guide     sanitation     practices  ,   and     reduce     recall     incidents  .   Policymakers     must     establish     clear     regulations  ,   standardized   sampling   protocols  ,   and     reporting     requirements     to     ensure     industry-wide     compliance  .   Strengthening   EMP   implementation     protects     consumers  ,   minimizes     outbreaks  ,   and     safeguards     international     trade     by     demonstrating     robust     preventive     controls     and     commitment     to     food     safety     excellence  .
